summary: Root Bubble Tea model that owns all sub-views and coordinates storage, refresh, and summarize operations

requires: optional RefreshService and SummarizeService via AppOption
guarantees: Implements tea.Model; initial view is dashboard; error messages auto-clear after 5s

state: Mutable value type (App); holds loading flag, active view, error + error ID, last refresh timestamp, and references to all sub-models

observations.text: Sub-view commands are eagerly executed inline via result := cmd() and pattern-matched, rather than being returned to the Bubble Tea runtime for dispatch. This breaks normal BubbleTea message routing for loadSummariesMsg, deleteChannelMsg, and addSourceMsg produced by sub-views.
Error auto-clear uses an incrementing errID to avoid stale clears: a tea.Tick fires after 5s carrying the ID at time of error; if a newer error arrived the ID will not match and the clear is ignored.
Manual refresh (r key) uses refreshFilteredCmd which passes the current dashboard filter; auto-refresh tick always calls refreshCmd (unfiltered RefreshAll). These are intentionally different.
Functional options (AppOption) pattern used for optional RefreshService and SummarizeService, keeping the constructor minimal for tests without those capabilities.

Directly accesses storage.Store in command closures (loadSummariesCmd, loadChannelsCmd, deleteChannelCmd, addSourceCmd); all DB calls happen in commands, not in Update, preserving the pure-function Elm architecture contract.

dashboard.filter is read in refreshFilteredCmd/summarizeFilteredCmd but channels.channels is also read at command-execution time — both are stale snapshots captured when the key is pressed, not at command execution. Filter may drift if channels were loading concurrently.
filterOverlayConfirmMsg and filterOverlayCancelMsg are forwarded directly to dashboard.Update — App intercepts them at the top level but delegates handling to the sub-model.
